Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- optimize: Fully autonomous RVV optimization loop for libnn functions. Target VPU, toolchain, build flags, FPGA board, and hardware profile all come from config.toml — no hardcoded paths or CPU names. Runs a 7-step FSM — validate, select, analyze, implement, verify, measure, learn — then loops back to analyze with new insights. No human intervention required.
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- optimize: Fully autonomous RVV optimization loop for libnn functions. Target VPU, toolchain, build flags, FPGA board, and hardware profile all come from config.toml — no hardcoded paths or CPU names. Runs a 7-step FSM — validate, select, analyze, implement, verify, measure, learn — then loops back to analyze with new insights. No human intervention required.
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Round 4 WIN: 576053 -> 574972 cyc (-1081 cyc, -2400 instrs). Cumulative 1.0020x. Committing and applying same fusion to remaining 3 sites in round 5.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash << 'OUTER'
LIB_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
SCRIPT_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/.claude/skills/optimize/script"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/parse_config.sh"
python3 - "${CFG_STATE_FILE}" <<'PYEOF'
import json,sys
from datetime import datetime
f=sys.argv[1]
s=json.load(open(f))
hist=s.get("round_history",[])
hist.append({
  "round":4,"approach":"Fuse vmul.vx + vadd.vx(NN_ROUND) into vmadd.vx + 1 vmv.v.x; reuse V30 for NN_ROUND broadcast",
  "cycles":"574972","instrs":"390360","ipc":"0.68","speedup":"1.0019",
  "outcome":"improved",
  "insight":"Saved 2400 instrs (-3 instrs/cur_row) and 1081 cyc (~0.19%). Eliminates 3-stage vsra->vmul->vadd RAW chain into 2-stage vsra->vmadd. Matches prediction."
})
s["round_history"]=hist
s["cycles_after"]="574972"
s["instrs_after"]="390360"
s["speedup"]="1.0020"
json.dump(s,open(f,"w"),indent=2)
PYEOF
git -C "${LIB_ROOT}" add Source/NNSupportFunctions/nn_mat_mul_kernel_u8_bias_2sft.c
git -C "${LIB_ROOT}" commit -m "$(cat <<'COMMIT'
[opt/rvv] conv_HWC_u8_u8_s8_sym_bias_fast: r4 fuse vmul+vadd(round) into vmadd (1.0020x)

nn_mat_mul_kernel_u8_bias_2sft.c unroll4 path lines 525-533: replace
4 vmul.vx + 4 vadd.vx(NN_ROUND) with 4 vmadd.vx + 1 vmv.v.x. Reuse V30
(holding bias, dead after the bias-add chain) as broadcast NN_ROUND vector.

vmadd.vx vd, rs1, vs2 = (rs1 * vd) + vs2 = out_scale*pre_rshifted + NN_ROUND.
This collapses the 3-stage RAW chain (vsra -> vmul -> vadd) into 2 stages
(vsra -> vmadd) and saves 3 ops/cur_row.

FPGA: 576053 -> 574972 cyc (-1081 cyc); 392760 -> 390360 instrs (-2400, -0.6%).
1.0019x this round; cumulative 1.0020x vs r0 baseline 576115. Test passes 3/3.

Co-Authored-By: optimize-skill <noreply@anthropic.com>
COMMIT
)" 2>&1 | tail -3
OUTER

Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. - iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.

content.text: All sites fused. Now verify and measure.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash << 'OUTER'
LIB_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
SCRIPT_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/.claude/skills/optimize/script"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/parse_config.sh"
FN="conv_HWC_u8_u8_s8_sym_bias_fast"
ROUND=5
REF="refs/optimize-ckpt/${FN}/round-${ROUND}-pre"
SHA=$(cd "${LIB_ROOT}" && git stash create "optimize ${FN} round-${ROUND} pre-implement" 2>/dev/null)
if [ -n "${SHA}" ]; then git -C "${LIB_ROOT}" update-ref "${REF}" "${SHA}"; else git -C "${LIB_ROOT}" update-ref "${REF}" HEAD; fi
python3 - "${CFG_STATE_FILE}" "${REF}" <<'PYEOF'
import json,sys
from datetime import datetime
f,ref=sys.argv[1],sys.argv[2]
s=json.load(open(f))
s["round"]=5
s["hypothesis"]="Round 5: extend vmadd fusion to remaining 3 sites (unroll4 M2 tail + 2 in tail kernel)"
s["checkpoint_ref"]=ref
s["changed_files"]=["Source/NNSupportFunctions/nn_mat_mul_kernel_u8_bias_2sft.c"]
s["cycles_before"]="574972"
now=datetime.now()
s["step"]="implement"
s["history"]=s.get("history",[])+[f"{now.isoformat()} implement (r5)"]
s["prev_step_started"]=now.isoformat()
s["step_started"]=now.isoformat()
json.dump(s,open(f,"w"),indent=2)
PYEOF
/usr/bin/bash "${SCRIPT_DIR}/rebuild_single.sh" Source/NNSupportFunctions/nn_mat_mul_kernel_u8_bias_2sft.c 2>&1 | tail -3
echo "--- qemu ---"
/usr/bin/bash "${SCRIPT_DIR}/run_qemu_test.sh" conv_HWC_u8_u8_s8_sym_bias_fast 2>&1 | tail -5
echo "--- fpga ---"
/usr/bin/bash "${SCRIPT_DIR}/run_fpga_test.sh" conv_HWC_u8_u8_s8_sym_bias_fast 2>&1 | tail -10
OUTER

content.text: Round 5: 574972 -> 575369 (+397 cyc, noise-level regression - the extra sites are rarely exercised). Reverting r5 to keep only r4 win.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash << 'OUTER'
LIB_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
SCRIPT_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/.claude/skills/optimize/script"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/parse_config.sh"
REF=$(python3 -c "import json;print(json.load(open('${CFG_STATE_FILE}'))['checkpoint_ref'])")
git -C "${LIB_ROOT}" checkout "${REF}" -- Source/NNSupportFunctions/nn_mat_mul_kernel_u8_bias_2sft.c
/usr/bin/bash "${SCRIPT_DIR}/rebuild_single.sh" Source/NNSupportFunctions/nn_mat_mul_kernel_u8_bias_2sft.c 2>&1 | tail -3
python3 - "${CFG_STATE_FILE}" <<'PYEOF'
import json,sys
from datetime import datetime
f=sys.argv[1]
s=json.load(open(f))
hist=s.get("round_history",[])
hist.append({
  "round":5,"approach":"Extend vmadd fusion to M2 tail + tail kernel main + tail kernel M2",
  "cycles":"575369","instrs":"390360","ipc":"0.68","speedup":"0.9993",
  "outcome":"regressed",
  "insight":"Extra fusion sites are rarely-executed paths (M2 tail not hit at out_tensor_ch=128; tail kernel runs only 1x/conv). +397 cyc within FPGA noise but no benefit. Revert; keep r4 only."
})
s["round_history"]=hist
s["cycles_after"]="574972"
s["round"]=6
s["history"]=s.get("history",[])+[f"{datetime.now().isoformat()} reverted r5"]
json.dump(s,open(f,"w"),indent=2)
PYEOF
OUTER

content.text: I've explored 5 distinct uarch tuning hypotheses (SW-pipeline rewrite, bias-load hoist, vadd interleave, vmadd fusion, vmadd extension). One real win (r4: -1081 cyc, +0.19%); rest were neutral or regressed within FPGA noise. The kernel post-r4 is well-tuned for this perf shape. Finalizing.

input.content:
---
name: vmadd.vx fuses vmul+vadd(NN_ROUND) in requantize tail
description: In u8/s8 mat-mul kernel requantize tails, vmadd.vx vd, scale, V_round replaces vmul.vx + vadd.vx(NN_ROUND), saving 3 instrs/cur_row and collapsing a 3-stage RAW chain into 2 stages. Reuse the bias-load reg (V30) as the NN_ROUND broadcast vector.
type: feedback
---

In libnn mat-mul kernels with the symmetric requantize pattern `acc = max(out_scale * (acc + bias) >> pre_rshift + NN_ROUND, 0) >> post_rshift`, the post-bias requantize tail typically emits:

```
4 vsra.vx  Vi, Vi, pre_rshift
4 vmul.vx  Vi, Vi, out_scale
4 vadd.vx  Vi, Vi, NN_ROUND
4 vmax.vx  Vi, Vi, 0
...
```

This can be fused into:

```
1 vmv.v.x  V30, NN_ROUND        // V30 is dead after the bias add
4 vsra.vx  Vi, Vi, pre_rshift
4 vmadd.vx Vi, out_scale, V30   // Vi = out_scale*Vi + V30
4 vmax.vx  Vi, Vi, 0
...
```

**Wins:**
- -3 instrs/cur_row (8 -> 5)
- 3-stage RAW chain (vsra -> vmul -> vadd) collapses to 2 stages (vsra -> vmadd) — saves ~3 cyc latency on the critical path per cur_row.
- Reuses V30 (bias holder, dead after the bias-add chain), so no extra register pressure.

**Why:** vmadd.vx vd, rs1, vs2 = (rs1 * vd) + vs2. Treating Vi as the multiplicand and V30 (broadcast NN_ROUND) as the addend computes out_scale * pre_rshifted_acc + NN_ROUND in a single instruction.

**How to apply:** Look for the vmul.vx ... + vadd.vx ...(NN_ROUND(...)) pattern in any u8/s8/s16 requantize tail. Confirmed +0.19% (1081 cyc, -2400 instrs) on `conv_HWC_u8_u8_s8_sym_bias_fast` r4 (2026-04-28). Apply ONLY at hot-path sites (unroll4 main loop) — extending to rare paths (M2 tail blocks, single-call non-unroll4 tail kernel) is noise-level (regressed +397 cyc in r5).

**Pre-condition:** A free e32 m1 vector register that's dead by this point. V30 (bias load) typically fits because bias is consumed by the vadd before the requantize chain.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Memory Index

- [AX45MPV vd4dots throughput](hw_ax45mpv_vd4dots_throughput.md) — vd4dots issues at 1/cyc; VW tail is latency, not throughput.
- [Pipeline-tuning predictions](feedback_pipeline_tuning_predictions.md) — V1024_8MB tuning page predicts inner-loop ceiling, not function ceiling; weight by inner-loop cycle share.
- [LMUL irrelevant for partial VL](feedback_lmul_partial_vl_throughput.md) — V1024_8MB VLSU 2*EMUL formula is worst-case-VL only; for vl<VLMAX_m1 m1==m4==m8 in cycles.
- [vle width unification breaks on int8 buffers](feedback_vle_alignment_unify_vsetvli.md) — vle8->vle32 to unify vsetvli for dual-issue fails: caller int8_t* not 4-byte aligned; QEMU hangs on misaligned vle32.
- [Clamp chains: batch vmax then vmin](feedback_clamp_chain_batching.md) — In requantize tails, batch all vmax then all vmin (not alternating) to hide VALU latency; measured +0.64% in unroll4 round 6.
- [llvm-mca for AX45MPV](reference_llvm_mca_ax45v.md) — Invocation flags for nx45v + Andes vdot/vqmac; what the model gets right vs wrong vs FPGA ground truth.
- [SW-pipeline amortization threshold](feedback_swpipe_amortization_threshold.md) — 4x4 vd4dots SW-pipeline pattern only nets positive when inner-loop iters >= ~4-5 per call; check `K/VLMAX` before porting from s8 sibling.

lines: - [Clamp chains: batch vmax then vmin](feedback_clamp_chain_batching.md) — In requantize tails, batch all vmax then all vmin (not alternating) to hide VALU latency; measured +0.64% in unroll4 round 6.
- [llvm-mca for AX45MPV](reference_llvm_mca_ax45v.md) — Invocation flags for nx45v + Andes vdot/vqmac; what the model gets right vs wrong vs FPGA ground truth.

- [SW-pipeline amortization threshold](feedback_swpipe_amortization_threshold.md) — 4x4 vd4dots SW-pipeline pattern only nets positive when inner-loop iters >= ~4-5 per call; check `K/VLMAX` before porting from s8 sibling.

+- [vmadd.vx fuses vmul+vadd(NN_ROUND)](feedback_vmadd_fusion_requantize.md) — In requantize tails reuse bias-load reg as NN_ROUND broadcast; vmadd.vx collapses 3-stage vsra->vmul->vadd RAW chain to 2 stages.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",

TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.

- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- optimize: Fully autonomous RVV optimization loop for libnn functions. Target VPU, toolchain, build flags, FPGA board, and hardware profile all come from config.toml — no hardcoded paths or CPU names. Runs a 7-step FSM — validate, select, analyze, implement, verify, measure, learn — then loops back to analyze with new insights. No human intervention required.

content.text:
Function #2 done with 5 rounds and 1 win.

**Final summary for `conv_HWC_u8_u8_s8_sym_bias_fast`:**

| Round | Approach | Cycles | Speedup | Outcome |
|-------|----------|--------|---------|---------|
| r0 | baseline | 576,115 | 1.00x | - |
| r1 | SW-pipeline rewrite (mirror s8 sibling) | 592,388 | 0.97x | regressed (reverted) |
| r2 | Hoist bias vle32 above slideup chain | 576,053 | 1.00x | neutral (kept) |
| r3 | Interleave vadd into slideup chain | 577,653 | 0.997x | regressed (reverted) |
| r4 | **Fuse vmul+vadd(NN_ROUND) into vmadd** | 574,972 | 1.0019x | **improved (kept)** |
| r5 | Extend r4 fusion to rare paths | 575,369 | 0.9993x | regressed (reverted) |
| HEAD remeasure | post-r4 clean | **574,609** | **1.0026x** | final |

ROADMAP updated. 2 commits on develop branch (r2 neutral hoist + r4 vmadd fusion). New memory entry: `feedback_vmadd_fusion_requantize.md` documents the proven peephole pattern for future siblings.
